mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes SL 280 / 320 / 350 petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned SL petrol engine typically costs £2,500–£5,500 supply only depending on generation. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. Main dealer new engines start from £8,000–£12,000+ supply only. M272 balance shaft repair costs up to £2,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes SL 280 / 320 / 350 petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which engine generation your SL has. The M112 (R129 SL280/SL320, 1998–2001) is widely regarded as one of the most reliable engines Mercedes ever made. The M272 (R230 SL350, 2005–2011) offers more power but early engines suffer from balance shaft issues. Check the Era Map above for your specific engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes SL 280 / 320 / 350 petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: Balance shaft failure on early M272 engines (R230 SL350) — requiring an engine-out repair, valve cover gasket leaks on the M112, crank pulley rubber deterioration on the M112, faulty accelerator pedal sensors on the M112, and ABC suspension issues on R230 models.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes SL 280 / 320 / 350 petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £6,000–£15,000 SL, a £3,500–£5,500 recon is 25–90% of the car's value. For a clean R129 M112 example, replacement is almost always worth it given the engine's legendary durability. For R230 M272 examples with balance shaft work completed, replacement is viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes SL 280 / 320 / 350 petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: R129 SL280 has M112.923 (2.8L V6, 204 hp); R129 SL320 has M112.943 (3.2L V6, 224 hp); R230 SL350 (2005–2008) has M272.966 (3.5L V6, 272 hp); R230 SL350 (2008–2011) has M272.968 (3.5L V6, 316 hp). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my M272 engine has the balance shaft issue?

acceptedAnswer.text: The balance shaft issue affects early M272 engines. Check your engine number — engines beyond the affected range are safe. The balance shaft gear teeth wear prematurely due to soft metal in the manufacturing process. If you hear rattling or see a check engine light, have the engine inspected immediately. A £1,500–£2,500 repair is far cheaper than a £5,500–£8,000 engine replacement.

eraMap.title: Mercedes SL 280 / 320 / 350 Petrol — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 2.8L V6 — 204 hp, 270 Nm. Mercedes' first V6. One of the most reliable engines ever made
3.2L V6 — 224 hp, 315 Nm. Bulletproof M112, smooth and durable
3.5L V6 — 272 hp, 350 Nm. Balance shaft issues affect early engines
3.5L V6 — 316 hp, 360 Nm. Revised engine; early 2006–2008 examples at risk

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes SL 280 / 320 / 350 Petrol Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an SL petrol engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). M272 balance shaft repair requires engine removal — a 22+ hour job.

problems.title: Balance Shaft Failure (M272, R230 SL350, 2005–2008)
problems.symptoms: Check engine light, rattling or knocking from the engine, timing chain noise, rough running, engine warning light
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–100,000 miles — the balance shaft gear teeth wear prematurely

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling or see the check engine light, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M272 balance shaft issue is the most critical fault on the R230 SL350. Early M272 engines (pre-2007) suffer from premature wear of the balance shaft gear teeth due to soft metal in the manufacturing process. If this issue is not fixed, it can lead to costly engine damage if the timing chain jumps. Engine numbers beyond the affected range are safe. At the first sign of rattling or a check engine light, have the balance shaft inspected immediately. A £1,500–£2,500 repair is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£8,000 engine replacement. Some owners report that the balance shaft issue affects only a small percentage of engines (5–10%).

failureLink.label: Read the full balance shaft failure guide → /failures/m272-balance-shaft/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Valve Cover Gasket Leaks (M112, R129 SL280/SL320)
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell, oil spots under the car, visible oil residue around the valve covers, low oil level warning
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice oil spots or burning smell

problems.recommendation: Valve cover gaskets are a common wear item on the M112 V6. They can be easily replaced as a DIY job. If you notice a burning smell or oil spots, have the gaskets inspected promptly. Catching leaks early prevents oil contamination of other components and more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide

problems.title: Crank Pulley Rubber Deterioration (M112)
problems.symptoms: Visible cracks in the rubber of the crank pulley, unusual vibrations, belt noise, potential loss of power steering or alternator charging
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice cracks or unusual noises

problems.recommendation: The crank pulley rubber can disintegrate on the M112 engine. This is not an expensive repair but should be addressed promptly. If you notice cracks in the rubber or unusual vibrations, have the crank pulley inspected.

failureLink.label: Read the full crank pulley failure guide → /failures/crank-pulley-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Faulty Accelerator Pedal Sensor (M112)
problems.symptoms: Delayed throttle response, particularly when warm; engine enters limp mode; poor acceleration
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — can occur on low-mileage examples

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if throttle response issues develop

problems.recommendation: The M112 and M113 are known for faulty accelerator pedal sensors. Symptoms include delayed throttle response, particularly when the engine is warm. The engine may enter limp mode with very slow revving. Replacing the pedal sensor is a relatively inexpensive fix. If you experience throttle response issues, have the pedal sensor checked.

failureLink.label: Read the full accelerator pedal sensor guide → /failures/accelerator-pedal-sensor/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: SL 280 / 320 / 350 Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Engine-out job; viable if caught early
Full engine replacement is the only reliable fix
Catch early to prevent belt failure

buyingChecks.title: Buying This SL 280 / 320 / 350 Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * R129 M112-engined SL280 or SL320 (1998–2001) — these are bulletproof
* R230 SL350 with engine number beyond the affected M272 range
* Post-2007 R230 SL350 (balance shaft issue resolved)
* Full service history with regular oil changes using MB-approved oil
* No check engine light or timing chain noise
avoidIf: * R230 SL350 (2005–2008) with no balance shaft history — this is a known weak point
* M272 engine with rattling or check engine light
* Poor service history — these are complex engines that need proper maintenance
* R230 with ABC suspension issues — these can be very costly

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The SL 280, 320 and 350 petrol badges span two very different engine generations. The R129 M112 (1998–2001) is widely regarded as one of the most reliable engines Mercedes ever made — "a great engine" and "one of the MB's most reliable platforms". The R230 M272 (2005–2011) offers more power but introduces the balance shaft issue on early engines. Buy an R129 M112 if you want bulletproof reliability. Buy a post-2007 R230 M272 if you want more power and modern refinement. Buy on documented history, not on generation alone.

cta.label: → Compare SL 280 / 320 / 350 Petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes SL 280, 320 or 350 Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Engine Number — For R230 SL350: check if the engine is within the affected M272 balance shaft range
* Cold Start — Listen for rattling or knocking from the engine (timing chain / balance shaft warning)
* Check Engine Light — Have codes been read? Look for timing-related codes
* Service History — Including which engine (M112 or M272) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the valve covers (M112)
* Throttle Response — Any delayed response or limp mode? (M112 pedal sensor)
* ABC Suspension — Check for sagging or compressor noise (R230 models)
* Rust — Check rear arches (R129 and R230)

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly.

items.value: SOHC, 3 valves per cylinder (18 valves total)

items.label: * Valvetrain
items.value: DOHC, 4 valves per cylinder (24 valves total)
